methylTracer 0.99.8
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Convert a methylTracer object to SingleCellExperiment

Source: R/met_to_sce.R
met_to_sce.Rd

Take a methylTracer object and convert it into a SingleCellExperiment where rows are markers/windows and columns are cells/samples. The main methylation matrix X is used as the assay (default name "meth"), and metadata stored in the HDF5 /obs and /var groups are mapped to colData and rowRanges/rowData, respectively.

Marker genomic coordinates are inferred from met@marker_name, assuming IDs of the form "chr_start_end".

met_to_sce(met, assay_name = "meth", scale_factor = NULL, verbose = TRUE)

Arguments

met

A methylTracer object.

assay_name

Character; name of the assay to store methylation values. Default "meth".

scale_factor

Numeric or NULL. If not NULL, the assay is multiplied by this factor (e.g. 100 to store beta values as percentages). Default NULL (no rescaling).

verbose

Logical; whether to print progress messages. Default TRUE.

Value

A SingleCellExperiment object with:

  • rows = markers/windows from met,

  • columns = cells/samples from met,

  • one assay (by default named "meth") containing methylation proportions in \([0, 1]\) or rescaled by scale_factor,

  • colData populated from the HDF5 /obs group,

  • rowRanges as a GRanges built from marker_name, with additional /var fields stored in mcols(rowRanges).

Contents

Developed by Zetian Jia, Xiang Li.
